scenario_results.reason: The package converted aligned appliance-service records into a bounded service status and next-step resolution note.
Missing model, serial, booking, and location facts produced a blocked disposition instead of an improvised answer.
Refund and pricing-exception requests were refused and routed with the relevant service and fee artifacts preserved.
Repeat-failure evidence triggered escalation rather than an unsupported replacement or remedy decision.
High-volume appliance-service terminology and queue pressure were handled without skipping evidence or safety controls.
Booking and service-fee conflicts were routed to the authoritative owner instead of being guessed in-lane.
The package rejected the unsupported closeout workaround and required the missing estimate and completion evidence.
Safety and account-mismatch signals stopped normal service handling and triggered a harm-aware escalation.
A service-status inquiry that became a guest-recovery complaint was converted into a clean handoff communication summary.
Missing tenant service-area and no-access rules produced a low-confidence escalation rather than an unsupported recommendation.
Conflicting OEM and tenant policy inputs were documented and escalated without autonomous fee or dispatch interpretation.
Tenant-specific scheduling constraints were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and authority boundaries.

notes: Scenario suite covers the required policy-exception, missing-detail, and handoff families plus booking mismatch, repeat failure, unsupported workaround, high-volume handling, safety or fraud escalation, and tenant adaptation.
All declared abilities for shared, workflow-phase, and retail-consumer-and-hospitality coverage map to passing scenarios.
